summary: Lead accepts M4 withdrawal and postwar handoff after writer R3 fixes, systems_architect ACCEPT-R3, and lead integration gates. Task remains ACCEPTED until the feature branch enters main; it is not CLOSED yet.

tests_run.result: Lead pre-accept R3 focused test passed earlier: 1 file, 11 tests.
Node/worker hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd, save bytes 10920.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism passed; root Vitest 32 files and 223 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file and 3 tests passed.
Prettier, ESLint, recursive typecheck, and architecture boundaries passed.
Task JSON validated in REVIEW before lead acceptance.
Diff limited to packages/protocol, packages/sim-core, tests, and allowed project task/message/route/model state files.

decisions: Objective-complete M4 handoff now requires completed campaign state, surrendered siege result, district target match, explicit marchId, and siege.marchId matching the resolved march before WarOutcome/PostwarCandidate creation.

Withdrawal records distinguish orderly, forced, cancellation, siege-lift, failed-extraction, and objective-complete flows without adding excluded tactical pursuit, naval evacuation, peace treaty, diplomacy, rebellion, UI, server, multiplayer, telemetry, or M5 content.
Orderly returned grain uses auditable M4 grain source ledger allocation with stable tie-breakers and conservation coverage.
WarOutcome/PostwarCandidate query DTOs copy nested arrays before exposure, and failed-extraction plus objective handoff negative paths have focused tests.
risks: Residual integration risk remains until PR CI passes and the branch is merged to main.
Save-format package remains intentionally untouched; persisted compatibility beyond current runtime state must be handled by an explicit save/schema task.
requested_action: Create PR, require CI success, merge to main, then close task only after main integration is verified.
